retry_reason: The put operation is unconditional and always runs when speq is non-NULL; after this call the object is freed, so retry would be a use-after-free.
contract_summary: Release wrapper for an arm_spe_queue object: decrements the reference count on the embedded thread (via thread__zput) and frees the decoder, event buffer, last branch, and the queue structure itself. After calling this function, the caller must not use the queue pointer again. No return value to check; the operation always succeeds for a non-NULL queue.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to release the reference on speq->thread via thread__zput and free associated resources; all other operations (decoder free, buffer frees) are cleanups tied to the queue's destruction, making this a focused put wrapper.

check.reason: thread__zput(speq->thread) decrements the refcount on the thread object. The access path starts from parameter priv (struct arm_spe_queue *), accesses .thread, then follows callee's path .orig.refcnt.refs. The type chain is consistent: arm_spe_queue->thread->original_thread->refcount_t->atomic_t. Segment counts match (5 each). Valid put operation.
